steps.title: Keep the phrase together
steps.content: The word "from" begins the prepositional phrase "from a collection of Norse tales dating to 1270." That phrase modifies "pages," so the preposition must stay directly connected to its object.

steps.title: Reject punctuation after from
steps.content: A, B, and D all place punctuation after "from," separating the preposition from its object. No punctuation is needed between "from" and "a collection of Norse tales." The answer is C.
